summary: Full backend + frontend smoke and integration testing for The Oxford Card Room landing. All 9 backend pytest cases pass against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Frontend renders hero, MICHAELMAS TERM badge, full 8-week timeline (timeline-week-1..8), three event cards from /api/events, Partners (Jump Trading) and Committee (Mirsaid Abdullaev, Atel Rassoli). RSVP dialog opens, submits successfully (success toast shown). Newsletter form submits successfully (success toast shown). Nav links scroll to their sections.
action_items: OPTIONAL/NAMING: Review request expected nav data-testids of pattern `nav-overview`, `nav-format`, etc. Implementation uses `nav-link-overview`, `nav-link-format`, etc. Functionally fine (all links clickable and scroll correctly), but if reviewer expects the exact `nav-{section}` pattern, add aliases.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `@app.on_event('shutdown')` is deprecated in FastAPI; prefer lifespan handlers (non-blocking).
server.py: POST /api/rsvp does not validate that event_id is one of the known event ids (boxing-night-2027, regular-poker-night, poker-lecture-series). Any arbitrary event_id is accepted and stored.
server.py: POST /api/rsvp and GET /api/rsvp are unauthenticated and unbounded — anyone can submit/read RSVPs including emails. Consider auth/rate-limiting before production.
server.py: created_at is stored as ISO string then parsed back on reads. Storing as native datetime via Motor would be cleaner and indexable.
server.py: CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — invalid combination per CORS spec; restrict origins for prod.
seed_data_creation: Tests insert TEST_ prefixed RSVP and example.com newsletter rows; not cleaned up (no delete endpoint). Low-volume, safe for review env.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use @example.com (not .test/.example) for EmailStr fixtures — pydantic email-validator rejects reserved TLDs. Nav data-testids are `nav-link-{label}` not `nav-{label}`. Event card testids: event-card-{id}; reserve button: event-rsvp-button-{id}. RSVP dialog: rsvp-dialog with inputs rsvp-input-{name,email,college,notes} and rsvp-submit-button. Newsletter: newsletter-input-email + newsletter-submit-button. Success toasts use sonner.
rca of the issue: Initial 3 backend failures were due to test fixtures using @oxford.test domain which pydantic's email-validator rejects as a reserved TLD. Fixed by switching test emails to @example.com — all 9 tests then passed. No production code defects found.
